rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory section as a meaningful comparator, not just a perfunctory literature list item. Larson and Hamilton explicitly place Nunes and Boatwright (2004) in prior reference-price work that increases willingness to pay via altered price perceptions, then contrast their own mechanism as operating through quality scaling and attribute weighting. This contrast helps frame the citing paper's claimed contribution and interpretive positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Nunes and Boatwright (2004) as prior reference-price evidence and then differentiates its own mechanism from that account. Specifically, it positions its contribution as showing increased spending from salient self-imposed restraints via quality-related processes rather than price-perception assimilation.
